# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm' # Caches npm dependencies

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --prefer-offline --no-audit

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Archive production artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist # Assuming 'dist' is your build output directory

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: build-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging # Links to a GitHub environment for secrets/protection rules

    steps:
      - name: Download build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: ./dist # Downloads the 'dist' artifact to this path

      - name: Deploy to staging server
        # Replace with your actual deployment command/script
        # Example using a placeholder:
        run: |
          echo "Deploying application to staging environment..."
          echo "Artifact content:"
          ls -F ./dist
          # For a real deployment, you might use:
          # scp -r ./dist/* user@staging.example.com:/var/www/html/app
          # Or call a deployment script:
          # ./scripts/deploy-staging.sh --artifact-path ./dist
          echo "Deployment to staging complete."
